page: Microsoft Azure F4ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine belonging to the Famsv6 family. It is powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor running at 3.7 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The server features 4 dedicated physical cores with 1 thread per core, providing 4 vCPUs. It is equipped with 32.0 GB of RAM, yielding a high memory-to-core ratio of 8.0 GB per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 32 KB of L1d cache, 1.0 MB of L2 cache, and 32 MB of L3 cache. The instance runs on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It does not include local storage or bundled GPUs, but it is premium storage capable.

Benchmark data indicates strong single-core performance, with stress-ng single-core CPU performance ranking in the top tier (top 10%) and Geekbench single-core scores in the top 25%. Single-threaded tasks like bzip3 decompression and text processing also demonstrate strong performance. However, multi-core benchmarks, including stress-ng multi-core and Geekbench multi-core, fall into the average range. A notable performance tradeoff is observed in memory bandwidth; while cached small-block read-only bandwidth is average, uncached large-block read-only memory bandwidth is weak, placing in the bottom 25%. Similarly, LLM inference prompt processing for medium (7B) models is weak, though text generation for small (135M) models remains strong.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the F4ams_v6 offers a dense memory allocation relative to its vCPU count, making it a viable option for memory-intensive workloads that do not require massive parallel compute. The absence of local storage means users must provision external premium storage, which should be factored into the overall resource design. This server is well-suited for database operations, caching layers, text processing, and single-threaded applications that benefit from high clock speeds and a larger memory footprint.
description: Microsoft Azure F4ams_v6 is a memory-intensive virtual machine from the Famsv6 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9V74 processor clocked at 3.7 GHz, paired with 32.0 GB of RAM (8.0 GB per core). While it lacks local storage and GPUs, it is premium storage capable. Performance benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU capabilities and strong single-threaded decompression and text processing. However, multi-core performance is average, and large-block uncached memory read bandwidth is weak, representing a key hardware tradeoff. Qualitatively, this configuration provides a cost-efficient memory-to-CPU ratio for workloads requiring high memory density without massive parallel processing. It is best suited for database operations, caching, single-threaded applications, and lightweight text processing.
og_description: Microsoft Azure F4ams_v6 is a memory-intensive VM featuring 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance, making it suitable for database operations and caching.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure F4ams_v6 is a memory-intensive VM with 4 dedicated AMD EPYC vCPUs and 32 GB RAM, optimized for database and single-threaded workloads.
tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC cores and high memory density for single-threaded and database workloads.

32.0 GB of RAM providing a high 8.0 GB per core ratio
Top-tier single-core CPU and strong single-threaded performance
No local storage included, but premium storage capable
Weak uncached large-block memory read bandwidth tradeoff
Best suited for database operations, caching, and text processing
